content.problemStatement:
Chef has recently started playing chess, and wants to play as many games as possible.

He calculated that playing one game of chess takes at least 202020 minutes of his time.

Chef has NNN **hours** of free time. What is the maximum number of **complete** chess games he can play in that time?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing a single integer, NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of complete chess games Chef can play in NNN hours.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the number of free hours Chef has.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum number of complete chess games Chef can play.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Each hour allows 3 games (60 minutes / 20 minutes per game). Therefore:
- N=1 → 1*3 = 3
- N=10 → 10*3 = 30
- N=7 → 7*3 = 21
- N=3 → 3*3 = 9
N=5 gives 5*3=15 games, N=9 gives 9*3=27 games.

solution.approach: Each chess game requires exactly 20 minutes. One hour has 60 minutes, so in one hour Chef can play 60 / 20 = 3 games. For N hours the total number of games is N * 3. The algorithm simply multiplies N by 3 for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
